steps.title: Recall the perpendicular lines rule
steps.content: When two lines are perpendicular in the coordinate plane, their slopes are negative reciprocals of each other.

steps.title: Extract line k's slope and find the perpendicular slope
steps.content:
From the equation $y = -\frac{17}{3}x + 5$, line $k$ has slope $m_k = -\frac{17}{3}$.

Apply the perpendicular slope formula:
$$m_j = -\frac{1}{m_k} = -\frac{1}{-\frac{17}{3}}$$

A negative divided by a negative gives a positive, so the negatives cancel:
$$m_j = \frac{1}{\frac{17}{3}} = \frac{3}{17}$$

The slope of line $j$ is $\frac{3}{17}$.
